item.description: It is a high stakes political week for President Donald Trump with primary elections that could indicate the future of the Republican party. A key test for Trump’s power is coming up in Kentucky. The Iran war has become a central political issue for Trump and Democrats argue voters will express their frustration at the ballot box. NBC’s Ryan Nobles reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: High-Stakes Primary Elections Test Trump’s Influence of GOP
media:content.media:description: It is a high stakes political week for President Donald Trump with primary elections that could indicate the future of the Republican party. A key test for Trump’s power is coming up in Kentucky. The Iran war has become a central political issue for Trump and Democrats argue voters will express their frustration at the ballot box. NBC’s Ryan Nobles reports for TODAY.

item.title: High-Stakes Primary Elections Test Trump’s Influence of GOP

item.dc:creator: Gary Grumbach
Alexandra Bacallao
Megan Lebowitz
item.description: Thousands gathered on the National Mall on Sunday for a day of prayer, worship music and patriotic speeches for an event celebrating the nation’s 250th anniversary that supporters hail as a public affirmation of faith in America but critics view as an exclusionary display that blurs the line between religion and politics.

media:content.media:text: A man holds both hands in the air with his eyes closed while praying.
media:content.media:description: Thousands turned out Sunday for “Rededicate 250," an event held on the National Mall in Washington that organizers billed as a chance to “solemnly rededicate our country as One Nation Under God.”

media:content.media:text: People sit on the grass and stand in front of an outdoor stage with the Washington Monument visible in the background.
media:content.media:description: Thousands turned out Sunday for “Rededicate 250," an event held on the National Mall in Washington that organizers billed as a chance to “solemnly rededicate our country as One Nation Under God.”

media:content.media:text: Two women raise their hands to the sky in prayer while others sit nearby on rows of folding chairs with the U.S. Capitol building visible in the background
media:content.media:description: Thousands turned out Sunday for “Rededicate 250," an event held on the National Mall in Washington that organizers billed as a chance to “solemnly rededicate our country as One Nation Under God.”

media:content.media:text: A diptych image shows attendees at "Rededicate 250: A National Jubilee of Prayer, Praise and Thanksgiving" in Washington on May 17, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Thousands turned out Sunday for “Rededicate 250," an event held on the National Mall in Washington that organizers billed as a chance to “solemnly rededicate our country as One Nation Under God.”

media:content.media:text: A man with a book over his face lying on the grass.
media:content.media:description: Thousands turned out Sunday for “Rededicate 250," an event held on the National Mall in Washington that organizers billed as a chance to “solemnly rededicate our country as One Nation Under God.”

media:content.media:text: "Rededicate 250: A National Jubilee of Prayer, Praise and Thanksgiving" in Washington
media:content.media:description: Thousands turned out Sunday for “Rededicate 250," an event held on the National Mall in Washington that organizers billed as a chance to “solemnly rededicate our country as One Nation Under God.”

media:content.media:text: A father and son stand with their backs to the camera wearing polo shirts with a white cross overlayed by the American flag.
media:content.media:description: Thousands turned out Sunday for “Rededicate 250," an event held on the National Mall in Washington that organizers billed as a chance to “solemnly rededicate our country as One Nation Under God.”

media:content.media:text: The U.S. Capitol building is visible behind a group of people praying
media:content.media:description: Thousands turned out Sunday for “Rededicate 250," an event held on the National Mall in Washington that organizers billed as a chance to “solemnly rededicate our country as One Nation Under God.”

item.title: Trump allies and Christian leaders kick off America’s 250th birthday with religious rally on National Mall

item.description: Organizers with “Freedom 250,” a group aligned with President Trump, said thousands of people turned out on Sunday for a massive prayer rally in the heart of Washington. Critics raised concerns about the lack of religious diversity at the event, with nearly every speaker and performer focused on Christianity. NBC News’ Gary Grumbach reports.

media:content.media:title: Thousands attend prayer rally in Washington
media:content.media:description: Organizers with “Freedom 250,” a group aligned with President Trump, said thousands of people turned out on Sunday for a massive prayer rally in the heart of Washington. Critics raised concerns about the lack of religious diversity at the event, with nearly every speaker and performer focused on Christianity. NBC News’ Gary Grumbach reports.

item.title: Thousands attend prayer rally in Washington

item.dc:creator: Sahil Kapur
item.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy’s primary loss on Saturday brings to an end a two-decade career in public office that was ultimately defined by tensions with President Donald Trump

media:content.media:text: Bill Cassidy.
media:content.media:description: Sen. Bill Cassidy at the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 15.

item.title: Sen. Bill Cassidy spent 20 years in public office. One decision led to his downfall.

item.dc:creator: Chantal Da Silva
item.description: The “Clock is Ticking” for Iran, President Donald Trump has warned, as deadlocked diplomacy and new drone attacks against U.S. allies in the Gulf fueled fears of a renewed outbreak of war

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-IRAN-US-ISRAEL-WAR-PROTEST
media:content.media:description: Women hold Iran's national flags during an anti-US and Israel protest at the Hafte Tir Square in Tehran on May 17, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Iran US
media:content.media:description: The Barakah nuclear power plant in Abu Dhabi's Western desert.

media:content.media:text: Iran And US Remain In Stalemate Over Strait Of Hormuz
media:content.media:description: A ship remains anchored on in the Strait of Hormuz near Larak Island, Iran, on Saturday.

item.title: Trump warns Iran ‘clock is ticking’ as new drone attacks hit U.A.E. and Saudi Arabia

item.description: The MV Hondius cruise ship at the center of the deadly hantavirus outbreak arrived in the Netherlands Monday where it will be disinfected. Meanwhile, a Canadian passenger has now tested positive as the 18 Americans from that ship remain in quarantine. NBC’s Maggie Vespa reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: Hantavirus Cruise Ship Arrives in Netherlands to Be Disinfected
media:content.media:description: The MV Hondius cruise ship at the center of the deadly hantavirus outbreak arrived in the Netherlands Monday where it will be disinfected. Meanwhile, a Canadian passenger has now tested positive as the 18 Americans from that ship remain in quarantine. NBC’s Maggie Vespa reports for TODAY.

item.title: Hantavirus Cruise Ship Arrives in Netherlands to Be Disinfected

item.dc:creator: Alexander Smith
item.description: There was increasing global alarm Monday over an Ebola virus outbreak in central Africa that the World Health Organization has declared a public health emergency of international concern

media:content.media:text: DRCONGO-HEALTH-VIRUS-EBOLA
media:content.media:description: Staff members at CBCA Virunga Hospital prepare rooms intended for possible suspected Ebola cases following official announcements in Goma, on May 17, 2026.

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-DRCONGO-HEALTH-VIRUS-EBOLA
media:content.media:description: A healthcare worker checks a visitor's temperature before allowing her access to a hospital in Goma on Sunday.

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-DRCONGO-HEALTH-VIRUS-EBOLA
media:content.media:description: A motorcycle taxi driver waits for clients in front of a hospital in Goma on Sunday.

item.title: New Ebola outbreak fuels mounting global alarm as U.S. works to relocate affected Americans

item.dc:creator: Nicole Acevedo
item.description: In Mexico, the girl’s condition has worsened and she’s not getting the specialized care she needs, her family said in an exclusive interview with NBC News.

media:content.media:text: A 10-year-old girl recovering from brain cancer, from the United States was deported with her undocumented parents last month.
media:content.media:description: A 12-year-old girl who is a U.S. citizen and is recovering from a brain tumor went with her undocumented parents to Mexico when they were deported last February. Her face has been blurred for her safety.

media:content.media:text: Immigration authorities removed four American citizen children from Texas, including a 10-year-old girl recovering from brain cancer, from the United States when they deported their undocumented parents last month.
media:content.media:description: A 12-year-old girl who is a U.S. citizen and is recovering from a brain tumor went with her undocumented parents to Mexico when they were deported last February. Her face has been blurred for her safety.

item.title: Humanitarian parole denied for deported parents of U.S. citizen girl with rare brain tumor

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
Nollaig O'Connor
Mosheh Gains
item.description: An air show at Mountain Home Air Force Base, Idaho, was abruptly canceled Sunday after two fighter jets collided in midair and crashed to the ground

media:content.media:text: A blurred photo of four pilots parachuting from two fighter jets after a mid-air collision.
media:content.media:description: A blurred photo of four pilots parachuting from two fighter jets after a mid-air collision.

item.title: Four aviators eject as 2 jets collide midair at Idaho Air Force base show

item.dc:creator: Gabriella Rudy
item.description: New York officials urged commuters to work from home Monday as a strike by Long Island Rail Road workers threatened to disrupt travel for hundreds of thousands of riders across the region.Gov.

media:content.media:text: Olivier Desinor leads striking workers in chants during a picket outside
media:content.media:description: Long Island Rail Road employee Olivier Desinor leads striking workers in chants during a picket outside Jamaica Station in Queens, New York City, on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: People exit a Long Island Rail Road train cart inside of a station
media:content.media:description: Long Island Rail Road employee Olivier Desinor leads striking workers in chants during a picket outside Jamaica Station in Queens, New York City, on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: People hold picket signs on the street outside
media:content.media:description: Long Island Rail Road employee Olivier Desinor leads striking workers in chants during a picket outside Jamaica Station in Queens, New York City, on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: People hold picket signs proclaiming a strike while marching on the street outside
media:content.media:description: Long Island Rail Road employee Olivier Desinor leads striking workers in chants during a picket outside Jamaica Station in Queens, New York City, on Saturday.

item.title: New York braces for chaotic Monday commute with Long Island Rail Road strike

item.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

media:content.media:title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
media:content.media:description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

item.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio

item.dc:creator: Rohan Nadkarni
item.description: What Jamal Crawford is about to tell you, he hasn’t even told his broadcast partners Mike Tirico and Reggie Miller yet

media:content.media:text: Three NBC sportscasters seated courtside during a basketball game.
media:content.media:description: From left, Reggie Miller, Jamal Crawford and Mike Tirico during a game between the Boston Celtics and the Minnesota Timberwolves at TD Garden in Boston in March.

media:content.media:text: Jamal Crawford dribbles a basketball with his left hand during an NBA game.
media:content.media:description: From left, Reggie Miller, Jamal Crawford and Mike Tirico during a game between the Boston Celtics and the Minnesota Timberwolves at TD Garden in Boston in March.

item.title: Jamal Crawford has to tone down his excitement while calling games for NBC

item.dc:creator: Alexander Smith
item.description: There was increasing global alarm Monday over an Ebola virus outbreak in central Africa that the World Health Organization has declared a public health emergency of international concern

media:content.media:text: DRCONGO-HEALTH-VIRUS-EBOLA

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-DRCONGO-HEALTH-VIRUS-EBOLA
media:content.media:description: A healthcare worker checks a visitor's temperature before allowing her access to a hospital in Goma on Sunday.

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-DRCONGO-HEALTH-VIRUS-EBOLA
media:content.media:description: A motorcycle taxi driver waits for clients in front of a hospital in Goma on Sunday.

item.title: New Ebola outbreak fuels mounting global alarm as U.S. works to relocate affected Americans

item.dc:creator: Nicole Acevedo
item.description: In Mexico, the girl’s condition has worsened and she’s not getting the specialized care she needs, her family said in an exclusive interview with NBC News.

media:content.media:text: A 10-year-old girl recovering from brain cancer, from the United States was deported with her undocumented parents last month.
media:content.media:description: A 12-year-old girl who is a U.S. citizen and is recovering from a brain tumor went with her undocumented parents to Mexico when they were deported last February. Her face has been blurred for her safety.

media:content.media:text: Immigration authorities removed four American citizen children from Texas, including a 10-year-old girl recovering from brain cancer, from the United States when they deported their undocumented parents last month.
media:content.media:description: A 12-year-old girl who is a U.S. citizen and is recovering from a brain tumor went with her undocumented parents to Mexico when they were deported last February. Her face has been blurred for her safety.

item.title: Humanitarian parole denied for deported parents of U.S. citizen girl with rare brain tumor

item.dc:creator: Chantal Da Silva
item.description: The “Clock is Ticking” for Iran, President Donald Trump has warned, as deadlocked diplomacy and new drone attacks against U.S. allies in the Gulf fueled fears of a renewed outbreak of war

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-IRAN-US-ISRAEL-WAR-PROTEST
media:content.media:description: Women hold Iran's national flags during an anti-US and Israel protest at the Hafte Tir Square in Tehran on May 17, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Iran US
media:content.media:description: The Barakah nuclear power plant in Abu Dhabi's Western desert.

media:content.media:text: Iran And US Remain In Stalemate Over Strait Of Hormuz
media:content.media:description: A ship remains anchored on in the Strait of Hormuz near Larak Island, Iran, on Saturday.

item.title: Trump warns Iran ‘clock is ticking’ as new drone attacks hit U.A.E. and Saudi Arabia

item.dc:creator: Julie Tsirkin
Scott Wong
item.description: Rep. Thomas Massie, a constant thorn in the side of GOP leaders during his eight terms in Congress, is no stranger to a primary challenge. But this year, President Donald Trump’s fury with the Kentucky libertarian has reached a boiling point.

media:content.media:text: Thomas Massie 11/12/25
media:content.media:description: Rep. Thomas Massie, R-Ky., talks with reporters outside the U.S. Capitol before the House voted on the Senate passed bill to reopen the government on Wednesday, November 12, 2025.

item.title: Rep. Thomas Massie confronts the full force of Trump’s wrath in a GOP primary

item.dc:creator: Patrick Smith
item.description: The hantavirus-stricken cruise ship arrived Monday in the Netherlands, ending a seven-week, 8,500 mile journey that was hit by up to 11 infections and three deaths from the rare disease

media:content.media:text: Netherlands Hantavirus Ship
media:content.media:description: The MV Hondius cruise ship arrives at the Port of Rotterdam, Netherlands, Monday, May 18, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Cruise Ship MV Hondius Arrives In Rotterdam To Be Disinfected Following Hantavirus Outbreak
media:content.media:description: The MV Hondius arrives at the Dutch Port of Rotterdam on Monday.

media:content.media:text: A cruise ship that sparked global alarm after a deadly outbreak of hantavirus docked in Rotterdam harbour on May 18, with the skeleton crew facing weeks of quarantine.
media:content.media:description: Health workers enter the MV Hondius wearing protective equipment.

item.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship arrives in Netherlands, ending long and deadly voyage

item.dc:creator: Gary Grumbach
Alexandra Bacallao
Megan Lebowitz
item.description: Thousands gathered on the National Mall on Sunday for a day of prayer, worship music and patriotic speeches for an event celebrating the nation’s 250th anniversary that supporters hail as a public affirmation of faith in America but critics view as an exclusionary display that blurs the line between religion and politics.

media:content.media:text: A man holds both hands in the air with his eyes closed while praying.
media:content.media:description: Thousands turned out Sunday for “Rededicate 250," an event held on the National Mall in Washington that organizers billed as a chance to “solemnly rededicate our country as One Nation Under God.”

item.dc:creator: Kayla Hayempour
Mark Hodge
Amina Kilpatrick
item.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy’s primary loss Saturday brings to an end a two-decade career in public office that was ultimately defined by tensions with President Donald Trump

media:content.media:text: Image: Sen. Bill Cassidy, R-La., left, speaks to supporters alongside his wife, Laura, during an election night watch party.
media:content.media:description: Sen. Bill Cassidy, R-La., left, speaks to supporters alongside his wife, Laura, during an election night watch party Saturday, May 16, 2026, in Baton Rouge, La.

media:content.media:text: Image: Israelis Observe Yom Ha'atzmaut, National Independence Day
media:content.media:description: Right-wing Nachala movement settlers march near the Gaza border near Kibbutz Nir Am, Israel, in April.

media:content.media:text: Photo illustrations of maggots alongside surgical equipment

item.title: Here’s the biggest news you missed this weekend

item.dc:creator: Mirna Alsharif
Austin Mullen
item.description: Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt was booed multiple times while discussing artificial intelligence during a commencement speech at the University of Arizona.

media:content.media:text: Eric Schmidt speaks
media:content.media:description: Eric Schmidt, chief executive officer of Relativity Space and former chief executive officer of Google, during the US-Saudi Investment Forum at the Kennedy Center in Washington, D.C., on Nov. 19, 2025.

item.title: Eric Schmidt booed during graduation speech about AI

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
Nollaig O'Connor
Mosheh Gains
item.description: An air show at Mountain Home Air Force Base, Idaho, was abruptly canceled Sunday after two fighter jets collided in midair and crashed to the ground

media:content.media:text: A blurred photo of four pilots parachuting from two fighter jets after a mid-air collision.
media:content.media:description: A blurred photo of four pilots parachuting from two fighter jets after a mid-air collision.

item.title: Four aviators eject as 2 jets collide midair at Idaho Air Force base show

item.dc:creator: Francie Ebert
item.description: Alex Cooper, 31, shared online she and her husband, Matt Kaplan, 42, are expecting their first child together.

media:content.media:text: Alex Cooper, left, and Matt Kaplan pose for a photo together
media:content.media:description: Alex Cooper and her husband, Matt Kaplan.

item.title: Call her Mommy: Podcast host Alex Cooper is pregnant with her first child

item.dc:creator: Gabriella Rudy
item.description: New York officials urged commuters to work from home Monday as a strike by Long Island Rail Road workers threatened to disrupt travel for hundreds of thousands of riders across the region.Gov.

media:content.media:text: Olivier Desinor leads striking workers in chants during a picket outside
media:content.media:description: Long Island Rail Road employee Olivier Desinor leads striking workers in chants during a picket outside Jamaica Station in Queens, New York City, on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: People exit a Long Island Rail Road train cart inside of a station
media:content.media:description: Long Island Rail Road employee Olivier Desinor leads striking workers in chants during a picket outside Jamaica Station in Queens, New York City, on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: People hold picket signs on the street outside
media:content.media:description: Long Island Rail Road employee Olivier Desinor leads striking workers in chants during a picket outside Jamaica Station in Queens, New York City, on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: People hold picket signs proclaiming a strike while marching on the street outside
media:content.media:description: Long Island Rail Road employee Olivier Desinor leads striking workers in chants during a picket outside Jamaica Station in Queens, New York City, on Saturday.

item.title: New York braces for chaotic Monday commute with Long Island Rail Road strike

item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
item.description: Three people were killed and several others were injured when a driver crashed into multiple cars and pedestrians late Saturday in Oakland, California, authorities said

media:content.media:text: A close up view of an Oakland Police patch on the shoulder of a uniform
media:content.media:description: A patch for the Oakland Police Department during a regular season game between the Chicago White Sox and Oakland Athletics on July 2, 2023, at RingCentral Coliseum in Oakland, Calif.

item.title: 3 people killed, several others injured after driver crashes into crowd in Oakland

item.dc:creator: Bethany Heitman
item.description: If you need a work bag, this travel backpack has a laptop sleeve, plus room for a water bottle, gym clothes and more — and it’s 40 percent off right now.

media:content.media:text: This travel laptop backpack comes in 23 different colors — including black, navy, gray, pink and green.
media:content.media:description: This travel laptop backpack comes in 23 different colors — including black, navy, gray, pink and green.

media:content.media:text: This travel laptop backpack comes in 23 different colors — including black, navy, gray, pink and green.
media:content.media:description: This travel laptop backpack comes in 23 different colors — including black, navy, gray, pink and green.

item.title: Save 40% on this perfect-for-work travel laptop backpack
